company_type: Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) - Multifamily Residential Properties
segments: Southern California (Los Angeles, Orange County, San Diego)

dependencies: Cost and availability of labor and materials required to maintain properties
Third-party service providers for information technology, security and business services
Third-party management companies for property management services
risks: Increases in operating expenses due to inflation, tariffs, immigration issues or other cost pressures
Supply chain disruptions affecting cost of labor and/or materials
Trade disputes, tariffs, immigration issues, labor unrest, geopolitical conflicts creating inflationary pressures
Third-party service providers systems may contain defects or lead to cyber breaches

regulatory_risks: Rent control or rent stabilization regulations and eviction restrictions
Federal government considering imposing rent regulations on multifamily properties secured by government-sponsored debt
State and local fire and life safety requirements
Federal, state and local accessibility requirements (Americans with Disabilities Act, Fair Housing Act)
Laws and regulations requiring climate-related disclosures (SEC proposed rules, California legislation)
Federal, state and local laws relating to collection, use, and security of resident, tenant, employee data (California Privacy Rights Act - CPRA)
Potential privatization of Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac
REIT qualification requirements under Internal Revenue Code
State and local income, excise and franchise taxes
Compliance with financial covenants in credit agreements and indentures
Tax-exempt bond restrictive covenants and deed restrictions including affordability requirements
